steps.title: Locate the subject-verb boundary
steps.content: The blank sits between instrument (the last word of the subject “the strings inside the instrument”) and are plucked (the main verb phrase). The question is whether any punctuation belongs at that boundary.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb punctuation rule
steps.content:
Standard English does not allow punctuation between a subject and its immediately following main verb.
  • Choice A — 'instrument:': a colon between subject and verb violates this rule. ✗
  • Choice B — 'instrument': no punctuation; the subject connects directly to the verb, conforming to Standard English. ✓
  • Choice C — 'instrument—': a dash between subject and verb violates this rule. ✗
  • Choice D — 'instrument,': a comma between subject and verb violates this rule. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
